records.ety: [Cf. OE. scheltrun, shiltroun, schelltrome, scheldtrome, a guard, squadron, AS. scildtruma a troop of men with shields; scild shield + truma a band of men. See Shield, n.]

defs: That which covers or defends from injury or annoyance; a protection; a screen.
quotes: The sick and weak the healing plant shall aid, From storms a shelter, and from heat a shade.

defs: One who protects; a guardian; a defender.
quotes: Thou [God] hast been a shelter for me.

defs: The state of being covered and protected; protection; security.
quotes: Who into shelter takes their tender bloom.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Sheltered (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Sheltering.]

defs: To be a shelter for; to provide with a shelter; to cover from injury or annoyance; to shield; to protect.
quotes: Those ruins sheltered once his sacred head.
You have no convents . . . in which such persons may be received and sheltered.

defs: To screen or cover from notice; to disguise.
quotes: In vain I strove to cheek my growing flame, Or shelter passion under friendship's name.

defs: To betake to cover, or to a safe place; -- used reflexively.
quotes: They sheltered themselves under a rock.
There oft the Indian herdsman, shunning heat, Shelters in cool.
